response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately.

***

reach out to the local community and support the factory workers by shopping at Sarah's shop. The promotional week is a great opportunity to enjoy quality handmade products while also contributing to the local economy. A yard of fabric or a pint of milk can make a difference in the lives of these workers, and the discount ensures that customers can enjoy these items without breaking the bank. Milk is a staple in many households, and with the discount, it becomes more accessible.
